pageTitle.value: Patrice Chéreau and Michał Borczuch - Video

pageCaption.value: The late French director Patrice Chéreau described himself as “a slave to the text”. His protégé Michał Borczuch takes the opposite approach, with an “anarchistic” style of direction. “I wasn’t interested in somebody who was doing something similar to me,” Chéreau said.

WaPageTitle.value: Patrice Chéreau and Michał Borczuch - Video

description.value: Rolex Mentor and Protégé in Theatre, 2012 - 2013

text.value: The late French director Patrice Chéreau described himself as “a slave to the text”. His protégé Michał Borczuch takes the opposite approach, with an “anarchistic” style of direction. “I wasn’t interested in somebody who was doing something similar to me,” Chéreau said.

pageTitle.value: Jennifer Tipton and Sebastián Solórzano Rodríguez - Video

pageCaption.value: Jennifer Tipton, one of the world’s greatest lighting designers, declares that “light is the substance of our existence”. Her protégé, Mexico’s Sebastián Solórzano Rodríguez, agrees completely as he witnessed Tipton creating lighting for performances at theatres and opera houses all over the world.

WaPageTitle.value: Jennifer Tipton and Sebastián Solórzano Rodríguez - Video

pageTitle.value: Peter Sellars and Maya Zbib - Video

pageCaption.value: With a live performance and both Chicago and Beirut as the backdrop, Peter Sellars and Maya Zbib give surprising – and very different – explanations of the nature of art, as well as describing each other, their mentorship and how to change the world.

WaPageTitle.value: Peter Sellars and Maya Zbib - Video

pageTitle.value: Julie Taymor and Selina Cartmell - Video

pageCaption.value: Julie Taymor, famous for creating spectacular theatre, film and opera, wanted to mentor “someone I can have a dialogue with”. She found her in British director Selina Cartmell, for whom the mentorship is an ideal opportunity for the rare privilege of watching another director at work as Taymor creates a new opera, Grendel.

WaPageTitle.value: Julie Taymor and Selina Cartmell - Video

pageTitle.value: William Kentridge and Mateo López - Video

pageCaption.value: Visual arts protégé Mateo López is, thanks to his architecture background, “incredibly precise”, says mentor William Kentridge. In his Johannesburg studio, Kentridge pushes his protégé out of his “comfort zone”, helping him to develop a more spontaneous approach to creativity.

WaPageTitle.value: William Kentridge and Mateo López - Video

pageTitle.value: Trisha Brown and Lee Serle - Video

pageCaption.value: For young Australian dancer Lee Serle, a year in New York being mentored by one of the world’s greatest choreographers, Trisha Brown, is an opportunity to “go back to the source”, as both mentor and protégé search for originality, humanity and beauty of movement.

WaPageTitle.value: Trisha Brown and Lee Serle - Video

pageTitle.value: Zhang Yimou and Annemarie Jacir - Video

pageCaption.value: For Zhang Yimou, China’s creator of dazzling films, success is not about inspiration but the fruit “of hardships and efforts” as he meticulously masters every single scene. For his protégée Annemarie Jacir, working alongside him on set and in the editing room as he makes The Flowers of War, his supreme “visual sense” is the key to his rich and moving stories.

WaPageTitle.value: Zhang Yimou and Annemarie Jacir - Video
